CREATE TABLE intervals_test (id INT PRIMARY KEY, interval_val INTERVAL YEAR TO MONTH);
INSERT INTO intervals_test VALUES (1, interval '2-5' year to month);
INSERT INTO intervals_test VALUES (2, interval '0-0' year to month);
INSERT INTO intervals_test VALUES (3, interval '1-11' year to month);
INSERT INTO intervals_test VALUES (4, interval '5-0' year to month);
CREATE TABLE other_data (data_id INT PRIMARY KEY, data_value INTERVAL YEAR TO MONTH);
INSERT INTO other_data VALUES (1, interval '3-6' year to month);
INSERT INTO other_data VALUES (2, interval '4-8' year to month);
SELECT a.id, a.interval_val, b.data_value FROM intervals_test a JOIN other_data b ON a.id = b.data_id;
SELECT id, interval_val + interval '0-1' year to month AS adjusted_interval FROM intervals_test;
SELECT id, interval_val - interval '1-0' year to month AS decremented_interval FROM intervals_test WHERE id > 1;
SELECT id, interval_val * 2 AS multiplied_interval FROM intervals_test;
SELECT id, interval_val / 2 AS divided_interval FROM intervals_test WHERE id = 3;
SELECT a.id, a.interval_val, (SELECT SUM(b.data_value) FROM other_data b WHERE b.data_id = a.id) AS total_data_value FROM intervals_test a;
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM intervals_test) AS total_intervals, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM other_data) AS total_data;
SELECT 'Total Intervals: ' || (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM intervals_test) || ', Total Data: ' || (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM other_data) AS summarized_data;
